title: Pentacalia danielis
intro: Pentacalia danielis, also known as Daniel's pentacalia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the Andes mountains in South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia danielis include flavonoids, sesquiterpenes, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Pentacalia danielis is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in the form of herbal teas, capsules, and tinctures to leverage its health benefits.
taxonomy: Pentacalia danielis is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Pentacalia, which is a group of plants that are closely related to the genus Eupatorium. The family Asteraceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Pentacalia danielis is native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant is a herbaceous perennial that grows in the wild in shaded to partially shaded areas.
